name: Mexico
contextBlurb: Mexico's Fintech Law (2018) created a framework for financial technology institutions including crypto exchanges, but Banco de México has explicitly restricted the use of virtual assets — including stablecoins — as means of payment within the regulated financial system. Private stablecoin issuance for domestic payments is not permitted, though crypto assets can be held as investments.

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Mexico's Fintech Law (2018) created a framework for financial technology institutions. However, Banco de México restricts virtual assets including stablecoins as payment instruments within the regulated financial system. Crypto can be held as investment assets.

regulators.name: Banco de México
regulators.role: Central bank. Has prohibited regulated financial institutions from offering virtual asset transactions to clients and restricts stablecoin use for payments.

regulators.name: Comisión Nacional Bancaria y de Valores
regulators.role: Banking and securities regulator. Co-supervises fintech companies and crypto exchanges under the Fintech Law of 2018.
